schema_rule: The filename holds the sample and the cooldown; the array axes hold the drive frequency (axis 1) and time (axis 2) together with the measured I/Q. Unlike the other experiments, the frequency axis is not stored in the file — it comes from generate_fig_13.py.

columns.name: cooldown_index
columns.description: Which cooldown the measurement belongs to — the variable this experiment compares. The two cooldowns are three days apart, with a thermal cycle to room temperature in between and no vacuum break or setup change.

columns.name: frequency_MHz
columns.description: Drive frequency, the primary swept variable: 3000 values from 2000 to 4999 MHz in 1 MHz steps. Each frequency gets its own single-pulse ring-down trace.

columns.name: timestamp_ns
columns.description: Time within the recorded trace, measured from t = 0 (start of the recorded window). The axis the ring-down physics lives on.

columns.name: I
columns.description: Raw in-phase homodyne signal, averaged over shots.

columns.name: Q
columns.description: Raw quadrature homodyne signal, averaged over shots. Combined with I to compute magnitude and phase.
